id: "13.4.3"
title: "GPU and TPU Utilization for Inference Workloads (DCGM, ROCm, cloud duty-cycle, Kubernetes allocation, OTel — SM-busy, memory pressure, latency-throughput, cost-per-token)"

# UC-13.4.3 · GPU and TPU Utilization for Inference Workloads (DCGM, ROCm, cloud duty-cycle, Kubernetes allocation, OTel — SM-busy, memory pressure, latency-throughput, cost-per-token)

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Performance, Capacity &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*Picture a taxi rank full of pricey sports cars rented by the hour. Sometimes every engine idles while riders wait in line; sometimes one car redlines while the others nap. We watch the rank so you neither waste money on parked horsepower nor let sudden crowds stall because nobody saw the fleet was lopsided.*

---

## Description

Detects under- or over-utilised NVIDIA, AMD, Google TPU, and AWS Trainium or Inferentia accelerators serving inference by fusing DCGM exporter metrics, ROCm SMI exporters, cloud duty-cycle telemetry, Kubernetes allocatable versus requested extended resources, and optional inference throughput or latency series, then enriching inventory and model catalog metadata for severity and FinOps context.

## Value

ML platform and FinOps leaders see which paid accelerators are idle, memory-bound, or batch-starved before latency SLOs break, while operators receive node-pool and MIG-aware guidance that separates harmless dev-cluster quiet from production waste and distinguishes tensor-active paths from misleadingly low SM counters.

## Implementation

Land DCGM, ROCm, cloud accelerator, kube-state, and inference exporter series into metrics indexes; normalize cluster, node, gpu_uuid, pool, and model_name; publish gpu_inventory.csv and catalog model_registry; save platform_uc_13_4_3_gpu_tpu_inference_utilization every five minutes with earliest=-4h@h; route critical and high severities to ML infrastructure on-call; archive weekly CSV exports for governance reviews.

## Evidence

Saved search platform_uc_13_4_3_gpu_tpu_inference_utilization on a five-minute schedule; observability dashboard drilldowns across DCGM mstats, ROCm prometheus, cloud duty metrics, kube allocation, and inference exporters; alert routing to ML infrastructure on-call; weekly CSV exports archived to a restricted evidence index.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

In a lab GPU node, induce sustained low DCGM_FI_DEV_GPU_UTIL while kube requests remain high on paid hardware, forward matching kube allocation and inference metrics, confirm platform_uc_13_4_3_gpu_tpu_inference_utilization returns critical or high with non-null recommended_action within two five-minute windows.

### Negative scenario

After restoring healthy batching and autoscale balance, when sm_busy_ma aligns with throughput_tokens_s, mem_used_pct is below pressure thresholds, and allocation matches steady requests, confirm severities return to info or low and the alert wrapper emits zero rows when filtering to critical only.

## Detailed Implementation

### Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with ML platform engineering, GPU infrastructure operators, Kubernetes platform teams, FinOps analysts, and the observability engineers who operate OpenTelemetry Collectors plus Prometheus federation tiers that land NVIDIA DCGM exporter series, AMD ROCm SMI derived gauges, Google Cloud Monitoring TPU duty-cycle signals, Amazon CloudWatch Neuron runtime metrics, and kube-state-metrics style allocation counters into Splunk metrics indexes. This use case answers whether expensive accelerators running model inference are genuinely busy in ways that move tokens, or whether they are idle, fragmented, memory-starved, or misconfigured slices that burn hourly commit contracts without throughput. It deliberately fuses hardware telemetry with Kubernetes scheduling truth and application-level latency-throughput curves so a single silent lane cannot hide a FinOps leak or a customer-visible queue stall.

The evidence plane spans five complementary metric families. First, NVIDIA Data Center GPU Manager exposed through the DCGM exporter publishes counters such as DCGM_FI_DEV_GPU_UTIL for SM occupancy style utilization, DCGM_FI_DEV_FB_USED for frame-buffer consumption, DCGM_FI_PROF_PIPE_TENSOR_ACTIVE for tensor-core activity proxies, and DCGM_FI_DEV_POWER_USAGE for electrical draw that contextualizes thermal and power-cap throttling. Second, AMD Instinct hosts expose ROCm SMI or rocm-exporter series that carry VRAM use, engine utilization, and error flags that must not be lumped into NVIDIA field names. Third, cloud hyperscaler consoles map TPU slices and Neuron cores into provider metrics like duty-cycle ratios and aws neuron runtime vCPU usage gauges that reveal when the control plane thinks the accelerator is active even if pod-level labels disagree. Fourth, Kubernetes kube-state-metrics or OpenTelemetry host receiver translations emit node allocatable accelerator counts and pod resource requests that expose fragmentation, pending pods, and device-plugin mismatches. Fifth, optional inference server exporters carry model_token_throughput and model_latency_ms histograms from Triton Inference Server, vLLM, Text Generation Inference, or Ray Serve so SM-busy percentages can be compared to token economics rather than guessed from GPU alone.

RBAC requires read-only service accounts for Prometheus scrape endpoints, OTEL receivers, cloud monitoring APIs, and Kubernetes API proxies. Break-glass kubeconfig files must never be pasted into saved searches. Legal and privacy reviewers should confirm whether model names, customer tenant labels, or internal cost rates may appear in lookup tables; redact or hash when policy demands. Network architects must validate that DCGM exporter sidecars on GPU nodes answer scrapes from cluster-local collectors without exposing metrics to the public internet.

Index design lands metrics in Splunk metrics indexes such as observability_metrics, prometheus_metrics, metrics, or otel with sourcetype=prometheus:metrics, sourcetype=otel:metrics, or native mstats-compatible metric names after OTEL translation. Logs from device-plugin startup failures may arrive as sourcetype=kube:events or container logs; route them to kubernetes or platform indexes for correlation. Retention should cover at least fourteen days hot for operational triage and thirty days for quarterly capacity attestations. Stamp change_ticket_id on maintenance events that touch drivers, container images, or MIG profiles so validators correlate benign churn.

Field normalization expectations: cluster should be a stable fleet key such as EKS cluster ARN short name, GKE cluster location tuple, or AKS resource group qualified name. node must be the Kubernetes node name that joins kube_node_status_allocatable labels, not only the cloud instance id unless you consistently alias. gpu_uuid should be the NVIDIA UUID, ROCm unique id, or cloud-assigned accelerator id that joins gpu_inventory.csv. pool should represent node pool, MIG strategy, or autoscaling group for baseline comparisons. model_name should align with catalog registry strings after canary aliases resolve. accelerator_type must distinguish nvidia_dcgm, amd_rocm, gcp_tpu_metric, aws_inferentia_metric, aws_trainium_metric, and unknown.

Risk briefing: low DCGM_FI_DEV_GPU_UTIL during heavy token traffic may indicate CPU-bound preprocessing, I/O stalls, batch-size one inefficiency, or an autoscaling policy that pinned too many replicas on one node. High DCGM_FI_DEV_FB_USED with moderate utilization suggests model weights plus KV cache pressure that will soon OOM-kill inference pods. Elevated DCGM_FI_PROF_PIPE_TENSOR_ACTIVE with low DCGM_FI_DEV_GPU_UTIL can appear on mixed-precision paths or when profiling counters roll differently across driver versions; never treat one gauge as gospel. MIG and MPS sharing change the meaning of utilization: a slice may show healthy internal load while sibling slices idle, or conversely aggregate node metrics may look hot while individual inference replicas queue. TPU duty-cycle metrics can diverge from host CPU saturation when the TPU worker is blocked on input pipeline stalls. Neuron core allocation mismatches show up as low duty with high request counts when batching rules disagree with compiler artifacts.

Differentiation recap: accelerator utilization and inference economics for UC-13.4.3, not UC-13.4.1 multi-vendor LLM HTTP latency and error-rate fusion, not generic CPU-only capacity cards, not security-only cryptomining detectors without capacity context.

Collector posture for DCGM exporter: run the exporter as a DaemonSet with hostPID and GPU volume mounts per NVIDIA documentation, or as a sidecar paired with the node problem detector only if you accept duplicated scrapes. Ensure nvidia-container-toolkit, driver version, and Fabric Manager on H100 NVSwitch topologies match the matrix published for your cloud. For MIG mode, export instance labels that include mig_uuid and profile names so Splunk rows do not collapse unlike accelerators. For time-slicing environments, annotate fractional requests in Kubernetes so allocated_pct math can reference both whole and shared devices.

AMD ROCm exporter deployment mirrors NVIDIA patterns but requires ROCm version alignment, rocm-smi visibility inside containers, and distinct metric names; do not alias Instinct MI250 and MI300 counters into A100 fields. Google Cloud TPU metrics require monitoring agent or OTEL googlecloud exporter permissions on tpu.googleapis.com read paths. AWS Trainium and Inferentia metrics require Neuron SDK versions that publish neuron_runtime_vcpu_usage and neuroncore utilization; map availability zone and instance type into gpu_inventory for hourly_cost_usd joins.

OpenTelemetry semantic conventions for hardware accelerators continue evolving; map device attributes such as accelerator.type, accelerator.id, and accelerator.name consistently at the collector so downstream mstats dimensions remain stable. Kubernetes device plugin documentation explains how allocatable counts change when Extended Resources are advertised; kube_node_status_allocatable for nvidia.com/gpu must align with node feature discovery labels after driver upgrades.

FinOps linkage: hourly_cost_usd in gpu_inventory.csv should reflect blended reserved and on-demand rates per pool, not list price alone, or severity will mis-rank idle dev clusters. cost_per_1k_tokens derives from hourly_cost_usd divided by throughput_tokens_s converted to hourly tokens, then scaled; document currency and tax handling outside Splunk.

SLO companion: mirror summarized outputs into a summary index keyed by cluster, pool, and model_name for multi-week burn reports when inference latency SLOs depend on accelerator headroom.

Extended operator education block: teach incident commanders that inference autoscaling tradeoffs differ materially across Ray Serve, vLLM, Triton, and Hugging Face TGI. Ray adds placement-group and actor scheduling layers that can leave GPUs idle while CPUs prepare batches; vLLM emphasizes continuous batching and paged attention so memory curves diverge from classic static batching; Triton exposes dynamic batching and model ensemble queues where GPU utilization may lag queue depth; TGI introduces router and shard patterns where token throughput must be summed across ranks. Mapping accelerator events to model bundle and version requires consistent labels on containers, Helm values, and catalog rows so joins resolve to bundle_version without ambiguous short names. Estimate cost-per-thousand-tokens by dividing fully loaded hourly_cost_usd for the serving pool by measured output tokens per hour from model_token_throughput, then multiply by one thousand; separate input and output pricing when FinOps policy demands blended weights.

Driver and firmware governance: maintain a matrix of tested NVIDIA driver branches against DCGM exporter versions and note that DCGM_FI_PROF_PIPE_TENSOR_ACTIVE availability depends on profiling field collections being enabled. Document ECC error handling paths because single-bit error counters may precede user-visible quality loss. NVLink and NVSwitch health checks belong in the same change calendar as CUDA and PyTorch upgrades to avoid topology surprises during torch distributed inference.

### Step 2 — Configure data collection

Stand up five complementary lanes the multisearch fans wide, plus CSV governance artifacts that keep ownership and hourly rates out of SPL literals.

Lane A, NVIDIA DCGM via Splunk mstats: land DCGM_FI_DEV_GPU_UTIL, DCGM_FI_DEV_FB_USED, DCGM_FI_PROF_PIPE_TENSOR_ACTIVE, and DCGM_FI_DEV_POWER_USAGE into a metrics index with dimensions host, UUID or gpu, kubernetes_cluster, node, pod optional, and exporter namespace. Confirm mstats queries return non-null _value across canary nodes before enabling alerts. When dual-writing Prometheus remote write and OTEL, dedupe scrape ownership to avoid doubled averages.

Lane B, AMD ROCm SMI exporter: scrape rocm-smi-exporter or amdgpu prometheus endpoints with labels for card serial, VRAM total, and temperature guardrails. Map metric names into prometheus:metrics rows with clear rocm or amdgpu prefixes. Validate Instinct MI300 cluster sees distinct power and utilization series versus MI250 baselines.

Lane C, cloud accelerator metrics: ingest Google Cloud Monitoring metrics for TPU duty cycle and AWS CloudWatch or OTEL aws exporter metrics for Neuron runtime usage. Normalize region, project, account, and accelerator family fields onto cluster and node aliases used in Kubernetes.

Lane D, Kubernetes allocation: scrape kube-state-metrics for kube_node_status_allocatable with resource label nvidia.com/gpu, google.com/tpu, aws.amazon.com/neuron, and kube_pod_container_resource_requests for the same extended resources. Include namespace and workload labels when policy permits for multi-tenant chargeback.

Lane E, inference application metrics: export model_token_throughput as a rate gauge and model_latency_ms as histograms from your inference stack. Ensure model_name labels match catalog registry spelling to allow join without fuzzy logic.

Lookup maintenance: publish gpu_inventory.csv with columns gpu_uuid, cluster, node, pool, owner_team, env, hourly_cost_usd, accelerator_family, and mig_profile. Refresh weekly from configuration management. Publish catalog sourcetype=model_registry with model_name, bundle_version, default_batch, and owner_team for join enrichment.

Example curl helpers from bastion hosts:

curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:9400/metrics | head
kubectl get nodes -o json | jq '.items[].status.allocatable'
aws cloudwatch list-metrics --namespace AWS/Neuron | head

props.conf guidance: set TRUNCATE for wide prometheus lines, honor prometheus timestamp when forwarded, and FIELDALIAS UUID to gpu_uuid when exporters vary.

Pre-save validation: confirm each lane returns rows in the last hour during steady inference, that a deliberate scale-down in lab produces a visible drop in throughput_tokens_s, and that kube allocation matches kubectl describe node for GPU counts.

Additional sampling discipline: configure relabel rules so high-cardinality pod ids never become unbounded metric labels on DCGM series; keep UUID and mig identifiers bounded per node. For multi-tenant clusters, segregate scrape jobs per namespace only when network policy allows collectors into each tenant slice.

### Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as platform_uc_13_4_3_gpu_tpu_inference_utilization with a five-minute schedule in steady production, dispatch.earliest_time=-4h@h, dispatch.latest_time=now, and alert when severity is critical or high after deduplication. Throttle duplicate cluster, node, and gpu_uuid tuples for twenty minutes unless severity escalates. Include recommended_action, accelerator_type, and sm_busy_pct in pager bodies so responders open DCGM dashboards, MIG profiles, or TPU slice consoles without guesswork.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch merges DCGM mstats, ROCm prometheus metrics, cloud duty-cycle metrics, Kubernetes allocation metrics, and inference server application metrics so correlated under-utilization surfaces even when one exporter stalls. bucket span=5m aligns series before stats computes sm_busy_pct, mem_used_pct, power_w, throughput_tokens_s, latency_p95_ms, allocated_pct, idle_seconds, and implied model_qps. streamstats window=6 approximates a thirty-minute moving average for SM-busy drift detection. eventstats establishes pool baselines for adaptive thresholds. case() encodes severity including critical for sustained low sm_busy_pct with meaningful allocation, high for memory pressure, medium for low allocation efficiency, and low for idle drift. coalesce() fills missing pod_name, model_name, and accelerator_type after vendor renames. inputlookup gpu_inventory.csv enriches owner_team and hourly_cost_usd. join to catalog model_registry adds bundle_version context for recommended actions.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.4.3 Accelerator inference utilization fusion DCGM mstats ROCm cloud duty-cycle Kubernetes alloc inference app earliest=-4h@h bucket=5m idle_sm=10 mem_hi=92 alloc_lo=35")`
| multisearch
    [ | mstats avg(_value) AS raw_val WHERE index=observability_metrics metric_name IN ("DCGM_FI_DEV_GPU_UTIL","DCGM_FI_DEV_FB_USED","DCGM_FI_PROF_PIPE_TENSOR_ACTIVE","DCGM_FI_DEV_POWER_USAGE") BY host, metric_name span=5m
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_cluster_name,"unknown_cluster"))))
      | eval node=lower(trim(toString(host)))
      | eval pool=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(nodepool,"default_pool"))))
      | eval gpu_uuid=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(UUID,strcat("nv_",host)))))
      | eval pod_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_pod_name,""))))
      | eval model_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model,model_name,""))))
      | eval accelerator_type="nvidia_dcgm"
      | eval sm_busy_pct=case(metric_name=="DCGM_FI_DEV_GPU_UTIL", raw_val, metric_name=="DCGM_FI_PROF_PIPE_TENSOR_ACTIVE", raw_val*100.0, true(), null())
      | eval mem_used_pct=case(metric_name=="DCGM_FI_DEV_FB_USED", min(100.0, raw_val/80000.0*100.0), true(), null())
      | eval power_w=case(metric_name=="DCGM_FI_DEV_POWER_USAGE", raw_val, true(), null())
      | eval throughput_tokens_s=null()
      | eval latency_p95_ms=null()
      | eval alloc_gpu=null()
      | eval req_gpu=null()
      | fields _time cluster node pool gpu_uuid pod_name model_name accelerator_type sm_busy_pct mem_used_pct power_w throughput_tokens_s latency_p95_ms alloc_gpu req_gpu ]
    [ search (index=prometheus_metrics OR index=metrics OR index=observability_metrics) sourcetype=prometheus:metrics earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(__name__,metric_name,""))))
      | where match(mn,"rocm_smi|amd_gpu|vega_smi")
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_cluster_name,cluster,"unknown_cluster"))))
      | eval node=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_node_name,instance,host,"unknown_node"))))
      | eval pool=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(nodepool,"default_pool"))))
      | eval gpu_uuid=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(card_serial,gpu_uuid,instance,"amd_gpu"))))
      | eval pod_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_pod_name,pod,""))))
      | eval model_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model,model_name,""))))
      | eval accelerator_type="amd_rocm"
      | eval raw_val=tonumber(coalesce(value,metric_value,"0"))
      | eval sm_busy_pct=if(match(mn,"util|gpu_use|busy"), raw_val, null())
      | eval mem_used_pct=if(match(mn,"vram|mem_used"), raw_val, null())
      | eval power_w=if(match(mn,"power"), raw_val, null())
      | eval throughput_tokens_s=null()
      | eval latency_p95_ms=null()
      | eval alloc_gpu=null()
      | eval req_gpu=null()
      | fields _time cluster node pool gpu_uuid pod_name model_name accelerator_type sm_busy_pct mem_used_pct power_w throughput_tokens_s latency_p95_ms alloc_gpu req_gpu ]
    [ search (index=prometheus_metrics OR index=metrics OR index=gcp_monitor OR index=aws_metrics) sourcetype=prometheus:metrics earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(__name__,metric_name,""))))
      | where match(mn,"tpu.*duty|neuron_runtime_vcpu|inferentia|trainium")
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_cluster_name,cluster,"unknown_cluster"))))
      | eval node=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_node_name,instance,host,"unknown_node"))))
      | eval pool=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(nodepool,"default_pool"))))
      | eval gpu_uuid=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(accelerator_id,instance,"cloud_accel"))))
      | eval pod_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_pod_name,""))))
      | eval model_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model,model_name,""))))
      | eval accelerator_type=case(match(mn,"tpu"),"gcp_tpu_metric", match(mn,"neuron|inferentia"),"aws_inferentia_metric", match(mn,"trainium"),"aws_trainium_metric", true(),"cloud_accel")
      | eval raw_val=tonumber(coalesce(value,metric_value,"0"))
      | eval sm_busy_pct=if(raw_val<=1.0, raw_val*100.0, raw_val)
      | eval mem_used_pct=null()
      | eval power_w=null()
      | eval throughput_tokens_s=null()
      | eval latency_p95_ms=null()
      | eval alloc_gpu=null()
      | eval req_gpu=null()
      | fields _time cluster node pool gpu_uuid pod_name model_name accelerator_type sm_busy_pct mem_used_pct power_w throughput_tokens_s latency_p95_ms alloc_gpu req_gpu ]
    [ search (index=prometheus_metrics OR index=metrics) sourcetype=prometheus:metrics earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(__name__,metric_name,""))))
      | where match(mn,"kube_node_status_allocatable|kube_pod_container_resource_requests")
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_cluster_name,cluster,"unknown_cluster"))))
      | eval node=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(node,instance,host,"unknown_node"))))
      | eval pool=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(nodepool,"default_pool"))))
      | eval resource=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(resource,""))))
      | where match(resource,"nvidia.com/gpu|aws.amazon.com/neuron|google.com/tpu|amd.com/gpu")
      | eval gpu_uuid=lower(trim(toString(strcat("k8s_",node,"_",resource))))
      | eval pod_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(pod,k8s_pod_name,""))))
      | eval model_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model,model_name,""))))
      | eval accelerator_type="kubernetes_alloc"
      | eval raw_val=tonumber(coalesce(value,metric_value,"0"))
      | eval alloc_gpu=if(match(mn,"allocatable"), raw_val, null())
      | eval req_gpu=if(match(mn,"requests"), raw_val, null())
      | eval sm_busy_pct=null()
      | eval mem_used_pct=null()
      | eval power_w=null()
      | eval throughput_tokens_s=null()
      | eval latency_p95_ms=null()
      | fields _time cluster node pool gpu_uuid pod_name model_name accelerator_type sm_busy_pct mem_used_pct power_w throughput_tokens_s latency_p95_ms alloc_gpu req_gpu ]
    [ search (index=observability_metrics OR index=prometheus_metrics) sourcetype=prometheus:metrics earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(__name__,metric_name,""))))
      | where match(mn,"model_token_throughput|model_latency_ms")
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_cluster_name,cluster,"unknown_cluster"))))
      | eval node=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_node_name,instance,host,"unknown_node"))))
      | eval pool=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(nodepool,"inference_pool"))))
      | eval gpu_uuid=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(accelerator_uuid,gpu_uuid,strcat("app_",host)))))
      | eval pod_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_pod_name,pod,""))))
      | eval model_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model,model_name,"unknown_model"))))
      | eval accelerator_type="inference_app"
      | eval raw_val=tonumber(coalesce(value,metric_value,"0"))
      | eval throughput_tokens_s=if(match(mn,"throughput"), raw_val, null())
      | eval latency_p95_ms=if(match(mn,"latency"), raw_val, null())
      | eval sm_busy_pct=null()
      | eval mem_used_pct=null()
      | eval power_w=null()
      | eval alloc_gpu=null()
      | eval req_gpu=null()
      | fields _time cluster node pool gpu_uuid pod_name model_name accelerator_type sm_busy_pct mem_used_pct power_w throughput_tokens_s latency_p95_ms alloc_gpu req_gpu ]
| eval cluster=coalesce(cluster,"unknown_cluster")
| eval node=coalesce(node,"unknown_node")
| eval pool=coalesce(pool,"default_pool")
| eval gpu_uuid=coalesce(gpu_uuid,strcat(node,"_accel"))
| eval pod_name=coalesce(pod_name,"")
| eval model_name=coalesce(model_name,"unknown_model")
| eval accelerator_type=coalesce(accelerator_type,"unknown_accel")
| bucket _time span=5m
| stats max(sm_busy_pct) AS sm_busy_pct max(mem_used_pct) AS mem_used_pct max(power_w) AS power_w max(throughput_tokens_s) AS throughput_tokens_s max(latency_p95_ms) AS latency_p95_ms max(alloc_gpu) AS alloc_gpu max(req_gpu) AS req_gpu values(accelerator_type) AS _accel_vals BY _time cluster node pool gpu_uuid pod_name model_name
| eval accelerator_type=mvindex(mvdedup(_accel_vals),0)
| eval sm_busy_pct=round(coalesce(sm_busy_pct,0),4)
| eval mem_used_pct=round(coalesce(mem_used_pct,0),4)
| eval throughput_tokens_s=round(coalesce(throughput_tokens_s,0),4)
| eval latency_p95_ms=round(coalesce(latency_p95_ms,0),2)
| streamstats window=6 global=f current=t avg(sm_busy_pct) AS sm_busy_ma BY cluster node gpu_uuid
| eventstats avg(sm_busy_pct) AS pool_sm_base BY cluster pool
| eval alloc_denom=if(coalesce(alloc_gpu,0)>0, alloc_gpu, 1.0)
| eval allocated_pct=round(min(100, (coalesce(req_gpu,0)/alloc_denom)*100.0),2)
| eval idle_seconds=if(sm_busy_ma<12, 1800, 0)
| eval model_qps=if(throughput_tokens_s>0, round(throughput_tokens_s/max(latency_p95_ms/1000.0,0.001),4), null())
| eval severity=case(sm_busy_ma<10 AND allocated_pct>40 AND mem_used_pct<70, "critical", mem_used_pct>92, "high", allocated_pct<35 AND sm_busy_ma>18, "medium", idle_seconds>0 AND sm_busy_ma<20, "low", true(), "info")
| eval recommended_action=case(severity=="critical", "right_size_replicas_review_batch_autoscale_and_mig_profiles", severity=="high", "free_kv_cache_add_capacity_or_shard_model", severity=="medium", "defragment_requests_tune_device_plugin_and_pool_topology", severity=="low", "scale_down_idle_nodes_or_rebalance_tenants", true(), "observe_next_bucket")
| join type=left max=0 gpu_uuid
    [| inputlookup gpu_inventory.csv
     | eval gpu_uuid=lower(trim(toString(gpu_uuid)))
     | fields gpu_uuid owner_team env hourly_cost_usd]
| eval hourly_cost_usd=round(coalesce(hourly_cost_usd,0),4)
| join type=left max=0 model_name
    [| search index=catalog sourcetype=model_registry earliest=-24h@h latest=now
     | dedup model_name
     | eval model_name=lower(trim(toString(model_name)))
     | fields model_name bundle_version catalog_owner]
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,catalog_owner,"unowned")
| table _time, cluster, node, pool, accelerator_type, gpu_uuid, model_name, sm_busy_pct, mem_used_pct, power_w, allocated_pct, idle_seconds, throughput_tokens_s, latency_p95_ms, hourly_cost_usd, severity, owner_team, recommended_action
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

[platform_uc_13_4_3_gpu_tpu_inference_utilization_alert]
cron_schedule = */5 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -4h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
action.email = 1
action.email.to = platform-ml-infra-oncall@example.com
action.email.subject = Accelerator util $result.severity$ $result.cluster$ $result.gpu_uuid$
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch platform_uc_13_4_3_gpu_tpu_inference_utilization | where severity IN ("critical","high","medium")

Performance: if Job Inspector shows multisearch queue time above objectives, materialize per-arm summaries into hourly summary indexes keyed on cluster, node, and gpu_uuid, widen alert windows on summaries, and retain this full search for investigations.

### Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins on the node: nvidia-smi dmon or rocm-smi monitoring during load tests should align directionally with DCGM_FI_DEV_GPU_UTIL and ROCm gauges within one bucket. Compare Kubernetes allocatable GPU counts with cloud console attached accelerators. For TPU workloads, validate duty-cycle charts in Google Cloud console against Splunk rows. For Inferentia, compare Neuron-top or neuron-monitor summaries with Splunk metrics.

Deep dive when sm_busy_pct is low yet latency_p95_ms is high: inspect preprocessing queues, tokenizer contention, network egress to object stores, and autoscaling lag before blaming accelerators.

Memory validation: when mem_used_pct approaches one hundred, confirm DCGM_FI_DEV_FB_USED derivatives against nvidia-smi memory used and verify KV cache growth settings in vLLM or Triton.

Negative test: after recovery when utilization is healthy, allocation matches requests, and latency is within SLO, confirm filtering severity to critical returns zero rows for six consecutive runs.

Runbook linkage: document approved driver upgrade windows so validators do not file defects during expected exporter restarts.

Chaos and load testing: inject single-pod failures, network partitions to object storage, and batch-size toggles in non-production while watching sm_busy_ma versus throughput_tokens_s to confirm the detection narrative matches lived experience.

### Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — Idle GPUs in a development cluster while hourly_cost_usd remains positive: confirm environment tags downgrade routing, verify autoscale scale-to-zero policies, and reclaim node pools after bake-offs complete.

Case 2 — MIG profile mismatch after driver upgrade: reconcile nvidia.com/mig.config label with actual mig.geometry, restart device-plugin pods, and reapply profiles under change control.

Case 3 — Model-loading hang holds VRAM without traffic: identify stale init containers or bad model URI mounts, bounce inference pods safely, and patch readiness probes to fail fast.

Case 4 — Triton versus vLLM saturation with divergent batching: compare dynamic batching windows, max queue delays, and concurrency limits; align prometheus scrape intervals to capture bursts.

Case 5 — Batch-size mismatch underutilising tensor cores: increase max batch tokens within latency budget, tune max_sequence_length, and validate continuous batching settings.

Case 6 — ECC errors silently degrading without process crash: run GPU health checks, migrate workloads off suspect nodes, and RMA hardware per vendor workflow.

Case 7 — NVLink or NVSwitch topology break causing torch distributed stalls: inspect fabric manager logs, verify peer GPU visibility, and rollback firmware changes tied to the incident window.

Case 8 — Kubernetes GPU fragmentation with pending high-priority pods: defragment via cordon-drain cycles, adjust request limits, or enable topology-aware scheduling plugins.

Case 9 — TPU host VM swap or disk pressure starving HBM feeds: resize host machine types, move datasets to local SSD, and tune input pipeline parallelism.

Case 10 — AMD ROCm runtime panic after ROCm bump: pin compatible PyTorch and ROCm pairs, collect rocm-smi exception traces, and roll back the train until validated.

Case 11 — Inferentia neuron-cores misallocation with low duty but high queue depth: recompile models for correct neuroncore groups, validate data parallel degree, and check security group rules for intra-instance traffic.

Case 12 — Cluster autoscaler oscillation thrashing GPU nodes: widen cooldowns, stabilize request-based metrics, and tie scale signals to queue depth plus utilization composites rather than CPU alone.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists DCGM mstats, ROCm, cloud, Kubernetes, and inference arms; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, case, inputlookup, and join appear; closing table includes cluster, node, pool, accelerator_type, gpu_uuid, model_name, sm_busy_pct, mem_used_pct, power_w, allocated_pct, idle_seconds, throughput_tokens_s, latency_p95_ms, hourly_cost_usd, severity, owner_team, and recommended_action.


## SPL

```spl
`comment("UC-13.4.3 Accelerator inference utilization fusion DCGM mstats ROCm cloud duty-cycle Kubernetes alloc inference app earliest=-4h@h bucket=5m idle_sm=10 mem_hi=92 alloc_lo=35")`
| multisearch
    [ | mstats avg(_value) AS raw_val WHERE index=observability_metrics metric_name IN ("DCGM_FI_DEV_GPU_UTIL","DCGM_FI_DEV_FB_USED","DCGM_FI_PROF_PIPE_TENSOR_ACTIVE","DCGM_FI_DEV_POWER_USAGE") BY host, metric_name span=5m
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_cluster_name,"unknown_cluster"))))
      | eval node=lower(trim(toString(host)))
      | eval pool=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(nodepool,"default_pool"))))
      | eval gpu_uuid=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(UUID,strcat("nv_",host)))))
      | eval pod_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_pod_name,""))))
      | eval model_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model,model_name,""))))
      | eval accelerator_type="nvidia_dcgm"
      | eval sm_busy_pct=case(metric_name=="DCGM_FI_DEV_GPU_UTIL", raw_val, metric_name=="DCGM_FI_PROF_PIPE_TENSOR_ACTIVE", raw_val*100.0, true(), null())
      | eval mem_used_pct=case(metric_name=="DCGM_FI_DEV_FB_USED", min(100.0, raw_val/80000.0*100.0), true(), null())
      | eval power_w=case(metric_name=="DCGM_FI_DEV_POWER_USAGE", raw_val, true(), null())
      | eval throughput_tokens_s=null()
      | eval latency_p95_ms=null()
      | eval alloc_gpu=null()
      | eval req_gpu=null()
      | fields _time cluster node pool gpu_uuid pod_name model_name accelerator_type sm_busy_pct mem_used_pct power_w throughput_tokens_s latency_p95_ms alloc_gpu req_gpu ]
    [ search (index=prometheus_metrics OR index=metrics OR index=observability_metrics) sourcetype=prometheus:metrics earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(__name__,metric_name,""))))
      | where match(mn,"rocm_smi|amd_gpu|vega_smi")
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_cluster_name,cluster,"unknown_cluster"))))
      | eval node=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_node_name,instance,host,"unknown_node"))))
      | eval pool=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(nodepool,"default_pool"))))
      | eval gpu_uuid=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(card_serial,gpu_uuid,instance,"amd_gpu"))))
      | eval pod_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_pod_name,pod,""))))
      | eval model_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model,model_name,""))))
      | eval accelerator_type="amd_rocm"
      | eval raw_val=tonumber(coalesce(value,metric_value,"0"))
      | eval sm_busy_pct=if(match(mn,"util|gpu_use|busy"), raw_val, null())
      | eval mem_used_pct=if(match(mn,"vram|mem_used"), raw_val, null())
      | eval power_w=if(match(mn,"power"), raw_val, null())
      | eval throughput_tokens_s=null()
      | eval latency_p95_ms=null()
      | eval alloc_gpu=null()
      | eval req_gpu=null()
      | fields _time cluster node pool gpu_uuid pod_name model_name accelerator_type sm_busy_pct mem_used_pct power_w throughput_tokens_s latency_p95_ms alloc_gpu req_gpu ]
    [ search (index=prometheus_metrics OR index=metrics OR index=gcp_monitor OR index=aws_metrics) sourcetype=prometheus:metrics earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(__name__,metric_name,""))))
      | where match(mn,"tpu.*duty|neuron_runtime_vcpu|inferentia|trainium")
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_cluster_name,cluster,"unknown_cluster"))))
      | eval node=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_node_name,instance,host,"unknown_node"))))
      | eval pool=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(nodepool,"default_pool"))))
      | eval gpu_uuid=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(accelerator_id,instance,"cloud_accel"))))
      | eval pod_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_pod_name,""))))
      | eval model_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model,model_name,""))))
      | eval accelerator_type=case(match(mn,"tpu"),"gcp_tpu_metric", match(mn,"neuron|inferentia"),"aws_inferentia_metric", match(mn,"trainium"),"aws_trainium_metric", true(),"cloud_accel")
      | eval raw_val=tonumber(coalesce(value,metric_value,"0"))
      | eval sm_busy_pct=if(raw_val<=1.0, raw_val*100.0, raw_val)
      | eval mem_used_pct=null()
      | eval power_w=null()
      | eval throughput_tokens_s=null()
      | eval latency_p95_ms=null()
      | eval alloc_gpu=null()
      | eval req_gpu=null()
      | fields _time cluster node pool gpu_uuid pod_name model_name accelerator_type sm_busy_pct mem_used_pct power_w throughput_tokens_s latency_p95_ms alloc_gpu req_gpu ]
    [ search (index=prometheus_metrics OR index=metrics) sourcetype=prometheus:metrics earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(__name__,metric_name,""))))
      | where match(mn,"kube_node_status_allocatable|kube_pod_container_resource_requests")
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_cluster_name,cluster,"unknown_cluster"))))
      | eval node=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(node,instance,host,"unknown_node"))))
      | eval pool=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(nodepool,"default_pool"))))
      | eval resource=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(resource,""))))
      | where match(resource,"nvidia.com/gpu|aws.amazon.com/neuron|google.com/tpu|amd.com/gpu")
      | eval gpu_uuid=lower(trim(toString(strcat("k8s_",node,"_",resource))))
      | eval pod_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(pod,k8s_pod_name,""))))
      | eval model_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model,model_name,""))))
      | eval accelerator_type="kubernetes_alloc"
      | eval raw_val=tonumber(coalesce(value,metric_value,"0"))
      | eval alloc_gpu=if(match(mn,"allocatable"), raw_val, null())
      | eval req_gpu=if(match(mn,"requests"), raw_val, null())
      | eval sm_busy_pct=null()
      | eval mem_used_pct=null()
      | eval power_w=null()
      | eval throughput_tokens_s=null()
      | eval latency_p95_ms=null()
      | fields _time cluster node pool gpu_uuid pod_name model_name accelerator_type sm_busy_pct mem_used_pct power_w throughput_tokens_s latency_p95_ms alloc_gpu req_gpu ]
    [ search (index=observability_metrics OR index=prometheus_metrics) sourcetype=prometheus:metrics earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(__name__,metric_name,""))))
      | where match(mn,"model_token_throughput|model_latency_ms")
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_cluster_name,cluster,"unknown_cluster"))))
      | eval node=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_node_name,instance,host,"unknown_node"))))
      | eval pool=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(nodepool,"inference_pool"))))
      | eval gpu_uuid=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(accelerator_uuid,gpu_uuid,strcat("app_",host)))))
      | eval pod_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_pod_name,pod,""))))
      | eval model_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model,model_name,"unknown_model"))))
      | eval accelerator_type="inference_app"
      | eval raw_val=tonumber(coalesce(value,metric_value,"0"))
      | eval throughput_tokens_s=if(match(mn,"throughput"), raw_val, null())
      | eval latency_p95_ms=if(match(mn,"latency"), raw_val, null())
      | eval sm_busy_pct=null()
      | eval mem_used_pct=null()
      | eval power_w=null()
      | eval alloc_gpu=null()
      | eval req_gpu=null()
      | fields _time cluster node pool gpu_uuid pod_name model_name accelerator_type sm_busy_pct mem_used_pct power_w throughput_tokens_s latency_p95_ms alloc_gpu req_gpu ]
| eval cluster=coalesce(cluster,"unknown_cluster")
| eval node=coalesce(node,"unknown_node")
| eval pool=coalesce(pool,"default_pool")
| eval gpu_uuid=coalesce(gpu_uuid,strcat(node,"_accel"))
| eval pod_name=coalesce(pod_name,"")
| eval model_name=coalesce(model_name,"unknown_model")
| eval accelerator_type=coalesce(accelerator_type,"unknown_accel")
| bucket _time span=5m
| stats max(sm_busy_pct) AS sm_busy_pct max(mem_used_pct) AS mem_used_pct max(power_w) AS power_w max(throughput_tokens_s) AS throughput_tokens_s max(latency_p95_ms) AS latency_p95_ms max(alloc_gpu) AS alloc_gpu max(req_gpu) AS req_gpu values(accelerator_type) AS _accel_vals BY _time cluster node pool gpu_uuid pod_name model_name
| eval accelerator_type=mvindex(mvdedup(_accel_vals),0)
| eval sm_busy_pct=round(coalesce(sm_busy_pct,0),4)
| eval mem_used_pct=round(coalesce(mem_used_pct,0),4)
| eval throughput_tokens_s=round(coalesce(throughput_tokens_s,0),4)
| eval latency_p95_ms=round(coalesce(latency_p95_ms,0),2)
| streamstats window=6 global=f current=t avg(sm_busy_pct) AS sm_busy_ma BY cluster node gpu_uuid
| eventstats avg(sm_busy_pct) AS pool_sm_base BY cluster pool
| eval alloc_denom=if(coalesce(alloc_gpu,0)>0, alloc_gpu, 1.0)
| eval allocated_pct=round(min(100, (coalesce(req_gpu,0)/alloc_denom)*100.0),2)
| eval idle_seconds=if(sm_busy_ma<12, 1800, 0)
| eval model_qps=if(throughput_tokens_s>0, round(throughput_tokens_s/max(latency_p95_ms/1000.0,0.001),4), null())
| eval severity=case(sm_busy_ma<10 AND allocated_pct>40 AND mem_used_pct<70, "critical", mem_used_pct>92, "high", allocated_pct<35 AND sm_busy_ma>18, "medium", idle_seconds>0 AND sm_busy_ma<20, "low", true(), "info")
| eval recommended_action=case(severity=="critical", "right_size_replicas_review_batch_autoscale_and_mig_profiles", severity=="high", "free_kv_cache_add_capacity_or_shard_model", severity=="medium", "defragment_requests_tune_device_plugin_and_pool_topology", severity=="low", "scale_down_idle_nodes_or_rebalance_tenants", true(), "observe_next_bucket")
| join type=left max=0 gpu_uuid
    [| inputlookup gpu_inventory.csv
     | eval gpu_uuid=lower(trim(toString(gpu_uuid)))
     | fields gpu_uuid owner_team env hourly_cost_usd]
| eval hourly_cost_usd=round(coalesce(hourly_cost_usd,0),4)
| join type=left max=0 model_name
    [| search index=catalog sourcetype=model_registry earliest=-24h@h latest=now
     | dedup model_name
     | eval model_name=lower(trim(toString(model_name)))
     | fields model_name bundle_version catalog_owner]
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,catalog_owner,"unowned")
| table _time, cluster, node, pool, accelerator_type, gpu_uuid, model_name, sm_busy_pct, mem_used_pct, power_w, allocated_pct, idle_seconds, throughput_tokens_s, latency_p95_ms, hourly_cost_usd, severity, owner_team, recommended_action
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Performance.cpu_load_percent) AS cpu_load latest(Performance.mem_used_percent) AS mem_used FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Performance.host
| rename Performance.host AS metric_host
| join type=left max=0 metric_host
    [| inputlookup gpu_inventory.csv
     | rename node AS metric_host
     | fields metric_host pool owner_team]
| join type=left max=0 metric_host
    [| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Inventory.cpu_arch) AS cpu_arch FROM datamodel=Inventory WHERE nodename=Inventory earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Inventory.dest
     | rename Inventory.dest AS metric_host]
| where match(lower(metric_host),"gpu|infer|tpu|trn|g6|p5|a100|h100")
| table metric_host cpu_load mem_used pool owner_team cpu_arch
```

## Visualization

Severity-colored table by cluster, node, and gpu_uuid with drilldowns to raw DCGM mstats rows, ROCm prometheus samples, cloud duty metrics, kube allocation lines, inference exporter histograms, and lookup-enriched owner_team; dual-axis chart of sm_busy_ma versus throughput_tokens_s; single-value tiles for mem_used_pct and hourly_cost_usd.

## Known False Positives

Chaos engineering or game-day exercises that drain inference traffic without scaling nodes down can show sustained low sm_busy_ma while finance still books hourly nodes; require change metadata and experiment tags before paging owners. Scheduled batch warming jobs that preload weights into VRAM may spike mem_used_pct while sm_busy_ma stays moderate until requests arrive; treat as benign when catalog marks the workload as warm-cache. Development and sandbox clusters intentionally park idle accelerators for researcher notebooks; downgrade severity using env labels in gpu_inventory rather than muting production logic. Model bake-off A/B routes that pin small canary fractions to large GPU pools can look under-utilised until traffic weight shifts; correlate with gateway weight tables. Evaluation harnesses that run intentionally slow single-batch precision tests will drag latency_p95_ms without implying customer traffic regression; join model_name to registry eval_suite flags when available. MIG reconfiguration windows can temporarily double-count UUID labels across old and new profiles until scrapes settle; verify mig_uuid stability across two buckets. Cloud monitoring ingestion lag during regional incidents can delay duty-cycle arms minutes behind live consoles; confirm pipeline health before declaring false negatives. ROCm or DCGM exporter upgrades restart sidecars and produce empty mstats slices for one interval; use streamstats dwell before critical pages. Neuron compiler version skew can emit misleading vCPU usage spikes during graph captures that self-resolve; inspect Neuron logs alongside metrics.

## References

- [NVIDIA DCGM Exporter (GPU telemetry)](https://docs.nvidia.com/datacenter/cloud-native/gpu-telemetry/latest/dcgm-exporter.html)
- [AMD ROCm SMI library documentation](https://rocm.docs.amd.com/projects/rocm_smi_lib/en/latest/)
- [Google Cloud — Tensor Processing Units (TPU)](https://cloud.google.com/tpu/docs)
- [AWS — Neuron (Trainium and Inferentia)](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/dlami/latest/devguide/inference-neuron.html)
- [Kubernetes — Device plugins](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/extend-kubernetes/compute-storage-net/device-plugins/)
- [OpenTelemetry — Hardware accelerator registry attributes](https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/attributes-registry/hardware-accelerator/)
- [Splunk Docs — Splunk Distribution of OpenTelemetry Collector](https://docs.splunk.com/observability/en/gdi/opentelemetry/opentelemetry.html)
- [NVIDIA DCGM user guide](https://docs.nvidia.com/datacenter/dcgm/latest/user-guide/index.html)
